description_long: This object is a vertically mounted red pennant, suspended from a thin black pole that is anchored to a flat, rectangular black base. The pennant itself is made of a fabric-like material, with visible horizontal ribbing or stitched lines running across its surface, giving it a textured appearance. The pennant is asymmetrical, with a slightly angular, irregular edge, and features a cut-out or slit near its center, adding visual interest and movement. The pole is slender and metallic, contrasting with the soft, flexible look of the pennant. The base is solid and heavy-looking, providing stability for the upright display. This type of pennant is commonly used for decorative or signaling purposes in urban environments such as parks, plazas, or event spaces.
description: A red, ribbed fabric pennant with a central slit, mounted vertically on a thin black pole and sturdy rectangular base. The pennant has an angular, asymmetrical shape and a textured surface.
description_view_0: The pennant is seen from the front, showing its full red surface, ribbed texture, and the supporting pole and base.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the depth of the pennant, the central slit, and the thinness of the fabric as it hangs from the pole.
description_view_2: This angle highlights the cut-out slit in the pennant and the way the fabric curves around the pole.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the back side of the pennant, with the ribbed texture and angular edge visible, as well as the supporting pole and base.
attribute_graphic_design: The pennant features a solid red color with horizontal ribbed lines and a distinctive central slit. There are no visible logos, text, or symbols.
